records.ety: [Pref. en- + grave a tomb. Cf. Engrave to carve.]

defs: To deposit in the grave; to bury.

records.ety: [Pref. en- + grave to carve: cf. OF. engraver.]
records.morph: [imp. Engraved (?); p. p. Engraved or Engraven (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Engraving.]

defs: To cut in; to make by incision.
quotes: Full many wounds in his corrupted flesh He did engrave.

defs: To cut with a graving instrument in order to form an inscription or pictorial representation; to carve figures; to mark with incisions.
quotes: Like . . . . a signet thou engrave the two stones with the names of the children of Israel.

defs: To form or represent by means of incisions upon wood, stone, metal, or the like; as, to engrave an inscription.
To impress deeply; to infix, as if with a graver.
